item.title: Secrets CLI — rotate and revoke commands
steps: Implement `secrets rotate <name>`: prompt for new value (masked), validate via health probe, atomically swap in .env (write to .env.tmp then rename), update rotated_at in .env.meta, emit audit event
Implement atomic .env swap in src/security/secrets/env.ts: write new content to .env.tmp with 600 permissions, then fs.rename() for atomic replacement

Implement `secrets revoke <name>`: remove entry from .env, verify removal by re-reading, scan all config files (openclaw.json, docker-compose.yml, workspace configs) for `${NAME}` references and warn if found
Implement dangling reference scanner in src/security/secrets/references.ts: glob config files, regex match `${SECRET_NAME}` and `$SECRET_NAME` patterns
Emit audit events for rotate and revoke (write to audit trail — consumed by FEAT-046)
Write unit tests for atomic swap, revocation, dangling reference detection
acceptanceCriteria: `clawhq secrets rotate MY_KEY` prompts for new value, validates, and atomically swaps in .env
Rotation updates rotated_at metadata and emits audit event
`clawhq secrets revoke MY_KEY` removes from .env and confirms removal
Revoke warns if config files still reference `${MY_KEY}`
Atomic swap uses write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern (no partial writes on crash)
